page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr6.8xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The instance is configured with 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs, providing a 1-to-1 thread-to-core ratio. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and network performance is anchored by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the ecs.hfr6.8xlarge demonstrates mid-range performance characteristics. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a single-core stress-ng score of 1436.69 and a multi-core score of 30656.07, while the Geekbench multi-core score reaches 11147.0. Memory subsystem performance is highlighted by a cached read-only small block bandwidth of 1704.61 GB/sec, which drops to 112.52 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, alongside a write bandwidth of 49.63 GB/sec. For database-related workloads, the instance processes 28.86 million Redis SET operations per second.

Lacking local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration, the ecs.hfr6.8xlarge relies entirely on network-attached storage and CPU-bound execution. This makes it highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local disk speed or parallelized GPU computing. The high memory density relative to compute resources makes this instance well-suited for in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, enterprise applications, and moderate data processing pipelines.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr6.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 3.1 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via KVM, it provides 256.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high 8.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying on a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external data transfers. Benchmark results place its performance in the average tier, with a multi-core Geekbench score of 11147.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 28.86 million operations per second. This hardware profile offers a cost-effective balance for memory-heavy, non-storage-intensive workloads. It is highly suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise application hosting.
